heading: Conversion or dismissal

L. 109–8section 1201 of this titlesection 1201 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: The debtor may convert a case under this chapter to a case under chapter 7 of this title at any time. Any waiver of the right to convert under this subsection is unenforceable.

children.label: b
children.text: On request of the debtor at any time, if the case has not been converted under section 706 or 1112 of this title, the court shall dismiss a case under this chapter. Any waiver of the right to dismiss under this subsection is unenforceable.
unreasonable delay, or gross mismanagement, by the debtor that is prejudicial to creditors;
nonpayment of any fees and charges required under chapter 123 of title 28;
failure to file a plan timely under ;section 1221 of this title
failure to commence making timely payments required by a confirmed plan;
denial of confirmation of a plan under and denial of a request made for additional time for filing another plan or a modification of a plan;section 1225 of this title
material default by the debtor with respect to a term of a confirmed plan;
revocation of the order of confirmation under , and denial of confirmation of a modified plan under ;section 1230 of this titlesection 1229 of this title
termination of a confirmed plan by reason of the occurrence of a condition specified in the plan;
continuing loss to or diminution of the estate and absence of a reasonable likelihood of rehabilitation; and
failure of the debtor to pay any domestic support obligation that first becomes payable after the date of the filing of the petition.

children.label: d
children.text: On request of a party in interest, and after notice and a hearing, the court may dismiss a case under this chapter or convert a case under this chapter to a case under chapter 7 of this title upon a showing that the debtor has committed fraud in connection with the case.

children.label: e
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, a case may not be converted to a case under another chapter of this title unless the debtor may be a debtor under such chapter.
